compiled_state.title: As an engineer running debug-everything, the skill re-scans GHA workflows and refreshes the open ticket list between fix batches so mid-session CI failures are addressed without a second run
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a between-batch GHA refresh step to debug-everything's Bug-Fix Mode — dispatching the same GHA scanning sub-agent defined in Story 1, placed after all tickets in the current batch have been attempted and before the Validation Mode gate.

**Why**: Newly-discovered CI failures (from fix-bug's anti-pattern scan, or from external CI runs completing during the session) get tracked and fixed in the current session rather than requiring a second debug-everything run. The skill also exits its bug-fix cycle without currently checking for tickets created mid-session, leaving newly-discovered bugs for the next run.

**Scope**:
- IN: Between-batch GHA refresh step in Bug-Fix Mode (after step 4 — all tickets attempted, before Validation Mode entry); re-query of open bug ticket list after the GHA scan; compact summary written as comment on active epic ticket (same behavior as Step 0); refresh runs regardless of debug.max_fix_validate_cycles value (including 0)
- OUT: Initial startup scan (Story 1); changes to max_fix_validate_cycles gate logic or Validation Mode behavior; changes to the sub-agent prompt (must reuse the shared prompt file from Story 1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode includes a between-batch step (after all tickets in the current batch have been attempted, before the Validation Mode gate) that re-runs the GHA scan sub-agent using the shared prompt from Story 1 and re-queries the open bug ticket list
  <- Satisfies: SC2 ("Between each fix batch in Bug-Fix Mode... debug-everything re-runs the same scan and refreshes the open bug ticket list")
- When this story is complete, the between-batch refresh runs when debug.max_fix_validate_cycles=0 — the refresh step is placed before the cycles gate so it executes regardless of the cycles setting
  <- Satisfies: SC2 ("regardless of debug.max_fix_validate_cycles setting")
- When this story is complete, any new CI failures discovered during the between-batch refresh result in bug tickets created via the tag-based dedup inherited from Story 1, and the refreshed open ticket list (including new tickets) is used for the Validation Mode decision
  <- Satisfies: SC2 ("creating tickets for newly-detected failures without matching tickets")
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  <- TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Tag-based dedup (gha:<workflow-file-name>) inherited from Story 1 prevents re-creating tickets for CI failures already tracked in the current session's Step 0 scan. Dedup operates across the entire session, not just the current batch.
- [Performance] When debug.gha_workflows is absent or empty, the between-batch refresh must short-circuit immediately (same "GHA scan skipped: no workflows configured" path as Step 0) without dispatching the sub-agent. Avoids unnecessary sub-agent overhead on every batch when GHA scanning is not configured.
- [Maintainability] Must reuse the shared GHA scanning sub-agent prompt file defined in Story 1 — do not duplicate the prompt inline in the between-batch step. The between-batch dispatch call should reference the same prompt file path.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed autonomously; pause and ask only when genuinely stuck or facing an unresolvable ambiguity. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.

comments.body: Fixed: Between-batch GHA refresh step added to Bug-Fix Mode — reuses gha-scanner.md, re-queries open ticket list, runs regardless of MAX_FIX_VALIDATE_CYCLES. 20 structural boundary tests pass.
comments.author: Test
